data.title: Vietnam Inflation Rate Rises to 4-Month High
data.description:
The annual inflation rate in Vietnam rose to 3.24% in May 2025, a four-month high, from 3.12% in April.

Price increases accelerated for textiles, footwear, and hats (1.14% vs 1.09% in April), housing and building materials (6.10% vs 5.73%), medicines and medical services (13.58% vs 13.56%), and other goods and services (6.64% vs 6.40%).

In contrast, inflation slowed for food and non-alcoholic beverages (3.72% vs 4.09%), while transport costs continued to fall (-5.66% vs -6.89%).

Meanwhile, core inflation, which excludes volatile items, rose to 3.33% from 3.14%, the highest since October 2023.

On a monthly basis, consumer prices increased by 0.16%, following a 0.07% rise in April.

data.title: Vice President JD Vance Says He Stands by Trump
data.description:
By Natalie Andrews

Vice President JD Vance was uncharacteristically silent for the day, as President Trump and Elon Musk feuded publicly. At about 10:30 p.m. Thursday night he posted on X: "President Trump has done more than any person in my lifetime to earn the trust of the movement he leads," he wrote on X. "I'm proud to stand beside him."

The tweet notably doesn't mention Musk. Vance also taped an episode of Theo Von's podcast, "This Past Weekend," that will air Friday morning.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Calix Says Unit to Scale Back Work on US Projects in Wake of Review of US Government Spending
data.description:
Calix said that its subsidiary business, Leilac, is scaling back work on its projects in the US pending the outcome of ongoing reviews of government spending by the administration of President Donald Trump, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

Leilac's projects were not impacted by the US Department of Energy's recent decision to terminate 24 awards that primarily funded carbon capture and sequestration and decarbonization programs, it noted.

The projects under review include those providing funding for Heirloom's direct air capture project, Project Cypress in Louisiana, as well as for two Leilac carbon capture and storage projects that were selected for awards for engineering studies.

The firm expects that the major project reviews may continue until the end of summer in the US.

Leilac and Calix are ready to increase capacity for project delivery in the US as soon as grant awards are confirmed or other paid customer projects are established, the filing added.

Calix's shares fell 2% in recent trading on Friday.

data.title: Ain Holdings' Profit Drops 19% in Fiscal Year Ended April
data.description:
Ain Holdings profit attributable to owners of the parent dropped 19% to 9.26 billion yen for the fiscal year ended April 30, from 11.4 billion yen.

The pharmacy and retail company's earnings per share (EPS) fell to 264.32 yen from 324.64 yen a year ago, according to a Tokyo bourse filing on June 5.

Net sales jumped 14% to 456.8 billion yen from 399.8 billion yen a year earlier.

It declared a final dividend of 80.00 yen per share for the year, payable from July 31.

For the fiscal year ending April 30, 2026, the company expects attributable profit of 11.45 billion yen, EPS of 326.46 yen, and net sales of 522.0 billion yen.

The company expects to pay a year-end dividend of 80.00 yen per share for the current fiscal year.

data.title: Cordlife Group Appoints Group CEO
data.description:
Cordlife Group appointed Chen Xiaoling as its group chief executive officer, effective June 5, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Friday.

Xiaoling will also serve as the CEO of Singapore, the filing added.

data.title: Cholamandalam Investment accepts bids worth 17.5 billion rupees for bond reissue, bankers say
data.description:
India's Cholamandalam Investment and Finance has accepted bids worth 17.5 billion rupees ($203.9 million) for the reissue of 7.38% May 2027 bonds, three merchant bankers said on Friday.

The company will offer a yield of 7.4271% on this issue and invited bids from bankers and investors on Thursday, they said.

The company did not reply to a Reuters email seeking comment.

data.title: Vietnam Retail Sales Slow in May
data.description:
Vietnam’s retail sales advanced by 10.2% year-on-year in May 2025, easing from an almost two-year high of 11.1% increase in the previous month.

The slowdown was driven by lower sales in tourism (35% vs 46.1%), services (14.3% vs 17%), and goods (8% vs 8.8%).

Meanwhile, trade continued to grow for accommodation and food services (19.1% vs 18.8%).

For the first five months of the year, retail activity expanded by 9.7%.

data.title: Malaysia Stock Market to See Portfolio Rebalancing — Market Talk
data.description: Malaysia's stock market is poised for portfolio rebalancing, after FTSE Russell announced that AMMB will replace Hong Leong Financial Group in the benchmark Kuala Lumpur Composite Index following its June semiannual review, say CIMB Securities analysts Ivy Ng Lee Fang and Lim Yue Jia in a note. The change, effective June 23, could trigger rebalancing activities by passive and index-tracking funds involving the affected constituents, they note. They expect AMMB to carry a higher KLCI weight of 2.7%, compared with Hong Leong's 0.7%. Historical trends suggest that newly added KLCI stocks often outperform. CIMB maintains its end-2025 KLCI target of 1560 and favors CelcomDigi, Public Bank and Tenaga Nasional. The KLCI is 0.1% lower at 1516.27. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: CapitaLand Ascendas REIT Issues New Units Under Private Placement to Raise SG$500 Million
data.description:
CapitaLand Ascendas REIT issued 202,430,000 new units at SG$2.470 per unit under a private placement, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Friday.

Trading on the new units commenced June 6, the filing said.

The trust raised around SG$500 million in gross proceeds from the placement and has utilized SG$5 million from the proceeds to pay the fees and expenses in relation to the placement.

data.title: Filled Jobs in Australia Fall 0.1% in March Quarter; Marking First Decline Since September 2021 Quarter
data.description:
The number of seasonally adjusted filled jobs in Australia fell 0.1% to 15.9 million in the March quarter, following a 0.3% increase in the prior three-month period, the Australian Bureau of Statistics said Friday.

Total jobs decreased 0.2% to 16.3 million, while job vacancies dropped 4% to 329,600.

"This is the first drop in filled jobs since the September quarter 2021, when lockdowns were introduced to combat the spread of the COVID-19 Delta variant," said Sean Crick, the bureau's head of labor statistics.

"Filled jobs have increased consistently since 2021, marking a total growth of 14.6% over the period," Crick added.

Hours worked rose 0.3% to 5.96 billion hours while average income per employed person lifted 1.4% to AU$24,755.10.

"Hours worked have continued to grow since the December quarter 2023, despite the impact of adverse weather events," Crick added.

On an annual basis, filled jobs rose 2% in the March quarter. Total jobs rose 1.7%, while job vacancies fell 9.4%.

data.title: Karns & Karns Announces Milestone of 2,500+ Five-Star Google Reviews, Demonstrating Industry-Leading Client Satisfaction
data.description:
Firm’s client-first approach and community commitment recognized through overwhelming positive feedback across California, Texas, and Nevada.

Michael and William Karns - Best Lawyers

LOS ANGELES, June 05, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) —  Karns & Karns Personal Injury and Accident Attorneys, a leading personal injury law firm founded by brothers Mike and Bill Karns, proudly announce a significant milestone: surpassing 2,500 five-star Google reviews. This achievement highlights the firm’s steadfast commitment to securing maximum compensation for clients while providing compassionate, client-focused legal support across California, Texas, and Nevada.

“The incredible feedback reflected in these 2,500 five-star reviews speaks volumes about the dedication and care our entire team brings to every client,” said Mike Karns, co-founder of the firm. “Each review tells a story of trust, resilience, and justice, affirming our belief in treating clients like family, not just case numbers.”

Bill Karns added, “Our mission goes beyond legal representation, it’s about positively impacting the lives of those we serve and the communities we are part of. This milestone celebrates our clients' victories and their trust in our relentless pursuit of justice.”

Karns & Karns combines aggressive legal advocacy with genuine empathy, understanding the physical, emotional, and financial toll accidents can take. The firm alleviates client burdens by managing every aspect of a case, from securing immediate medical treatment and rental car assistance to navigating complex insurance negotiations, so clients can focus fully on their recovery.

The firm provides expert legal assistance in a wide range of cases, including:

  • Motor Vehicle Accidents
  • Commercial Truck Accidents
  • Motorcycle Accidents
  • Bus Accidents
  • Pedestrian Accidents
  • Slip and Fall Injuries
  • Wrongful Death Claims
  • Rideshare Accidents (Uber/Lyft)
  • Sexual Abuse and Harassment Cases
  • Premises Liability and more

Beyond legal excellence, Karns & Karns actively supports philanthropy, reflecting its core values. The firm proudly backs the United Farm Workers, advocating for agricultural workers' rights and fair conditions. They also support wounded veterans through the 4 Paws for Patriots program, which provides highly trained service dogs to assist military personnel with PTSD and daily tasks.

Karns & Karns maintains a strategic network of over 40 office locations across California, Texas, and Nevada, ensuring accessible and timely legal support to a broad clientele. This expansive presence enables the firm to connect personally with clients in diverse communities while maintaining a strong local understanding of regional laws and courts.

For a free, no-obligation consultation or to learn more about Karns & Karns Personal Injury and Accident Attorneys, visit www.karnsandkarns.com or call 1-800-484-3946 (1-800-4THEWIN).

About Karns & Karns Personal Injury and Accident Attorneys

Founded by two of the best lawyers in personal injury law, brothers Mike and Bill Karns, Karns & Karns is nationally known for exceptional results and client-first representation. The firm specializes in a wide range of personal injury cases and is committed to securing maximum compensation while delivering compassionate legal support. With a strong presence across California, Texas, and Nevada, Karns & Karns makes a meaningful difference in the lives of its clients and the communities it serves.

data.title: India bond yields likely to open lower ahead of RBI decision
data.description:
Indian government bond yields are expected to open slightly lower on Friday ahead of the Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) policy decision, with an interest rate cut already factored in.

The yield on the benchmark 10-year bond (IN063335G=CC) is expected to move between 6.18% and 6.20% till the policy decision, a trader with a private bank said, compared with Thursday's close of 6.1960%.

The five-year 6.75% 2029 bond yield (IN067529G=CC) had ended last session at 5.8436%.

"(The) market is going into the policy with hopes of more than just a rate cut and if there is nothing else, we could see reversal of positions," the trader said.

The Reserve Bank of India is widely expected to cut rates by 25 basis points for the third consecutive time this year, as muted inflation provides ample space to focus on boosting economic growth.

The announcement is due at 10:00 a.m. IST.

The RBI has lowered rates by 50 bps so far in 2025 and infused $100 billion into the banking system between December and May.

Nuvama, which expects the central bank to cut its key lending rate by 25 bps, will watch out for any potential tweaks to the liquidity management or revision in guidance.

While most economists expect a 25 bps cut, the State Bank of India (SBI) said the RBI could slash rates by an outsized 50 bps to jumpstart the credit cycle.

Apart from the rate cuts, markets are also betting on the central bank introducing additional liquidity measures, which would support the shorter end of the yield curve.

Traders will focus on the upcoming debt sale, with the government aiming to raise 360 billion rupees ($4.19 billion).

RATES

Overnight index swap (OIS) rates are expected to move in a narrow band until the policy decision.

The one-year (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) OIS rate ended at 5.53% on Thursday, while the two-year OIS rate (INR2YMIBROIS=CC) closed at 5.41%.

However, the most liquid five-year (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) OIS broke below its key technical level to finish at 5.60%.

KEY INDICATORS:

** Brent crude futures fell 0.3% to $65.20 per barrel after easing 1.2% on Thursday

** Ten-year U.S. Treasury yield at 4.3906%; two-year yield (US2YT=RR) at 3.9139%

** RBI to announce monetary policy decision

** India to sell sovereign bonds worth 320 billion rupees

** RBI to set underwriting fees for 320 billion rupees of sovereign bond auction

** RBI to conduct three-day variable rate repo auction for 250 billion rupees

($1 = 85.8270 Indian rupees)

data.title: Elon Musk Burns MAGA Bridges as Republicans Back Trump in Brawl — WSJ
data.description:
By Olivia Beavers and Jasmine Li

WASHINGTON — Rep. Troy Nehls (R., Texas), a vocal President Trump defender, stared into television cameras that circled him outside the Capitol steps and addressed Elon Musk: "You've lost your damn mind."

"Enough is enough. Stop this," he said.

Several hours of public fighting between Musk and Trump — triggered by criticism of Trump's tax and spending bill — was quickly turning the billionaire megadonor from a revered MAGA figure to outright outcast among Republican lawmakers. The brawl, playing out on television and social media, risked derailing Musk's push to convince Republicans to overhaul their signature legislation, which he painted as a budget-busting abomination.

As the fight heated up, Musk claimed credit for Trump's 2024 win and also agreed with a social-media message saying Trump should be removed from office. Trump threatened to revoke Musk's government contracts and wondered if Musk suffered from "Trump derangement syndrome."

In the fight between the Republican titans, most GOP lawmakers sided with Trump. Rep. Tim Burchett (R., Tenn.) said Musk is "going off the deep end" with his call for Trump's impeachment and suggested Musk is losing his sway with the MAGA crowd. On whether Musk has power on the Hill anymore, Burchett replied: "You know, he's got a huge checkbook, obviously, and this town is gutless."

Many Republicans have defended the GOP megabill, which passed the House last month by one vote and is now being worked on by the Senate. Backers argue that the tax cuts, border money and spending cuts make it a good package they can support, even if they concede it isn't perfect. Musk's attacks had raised the possibility that conservatives could extract more cuts from such programs as Medicaid, but that new push appeared to stall as Musk's fight with Trump stretched deeper into Thursday.

"He hasn't moved a vote," said House Majority Leader Steve Scalise (R., La.) of Musk.

White House aides argued Musk's broadsides would weaken the position of Republican critics of the megabill, who had initially viewed Musk's spotlight on deficits as helpful to their cause.

Sen. Jim Banks (R., Ind.) characterized Trump as "taking the high ground" in the argument with Musk, saying the president is focused on passing the tax bill. In comments on CNN, he also credited Musk for his cost-cutting work and said he believes the Senate could address some of his concerns about deficits generated by the House bill by finding further savings.

House Speaker Mike Johnson (R., La.) said he has been trying to change Musk's mind on the legislation, texting him information after Musk didn't return his phone calls. Johnson called Musk a friend, but rejected Musk's claim that he should get credit for 2024 election wins by Trump and Republicans.

"I don't think any one person deserves credit, but if there is one person, that's Donald J. Trump," Johnson said.

While many Republicans backed Trump in the breakup, Musk still has his fans. Musk received public support from the two House Republicans who voted against the bill last month: Reps. Thomas Massie (R., Ky.) and Warren Davidson (R., Ohio).

Rep. David Schweikert, an Arizona Republican seen as vulnerable in the midterm elections next year, said on the debt: "Musk is absolutely right."

Some lawmakers likened the Trump-Musk feud to a painful divorce.

Rep. Aaron Bean (R., Fla.), chairman of the DOGE caucus in the House, said his "heart aches" over the dispute, and said lawmakers remained committed to the DOGE effort despite the crackup. "You see two friends fighting, and it's sad that it escalated so quickly," he said.

Bean said he hoped they could patch things up. "Maybe they can share a Diet Coke together."

The implosion of the relationship comes after weeks of simmering tensions, including Musk being miffed by the president's decision to withdraw a key ally of his to lead the National Aeronautics and Space Administration, Jared Isaacman. Musk had also raised concerns about provisions in the legislation that repeal tax credits for electric vehicles, according to people familiar with the matter, which would have impacted Musk's car company, Tesla.

Musk, on X, denied that the EV mandate facilitated his decision to criticize the bill, which he called "abhorrent," and argued would drive the U.S. further into debt.

The Congressional Budget Office, a nonpartisan body that provides budget and economic impact scores of bills to Congress, predicts the House GOP's tax bill would cut taxes by $3.7 trillion but raise the deficit by $2.4 trillion over the next decade.

Some lawmakers said they weren't surprised by the breakup.

"When you get two strong personalities, it's bound to happen," said Rep. Ralph Norman (R., S.C.), who called it "sad for the country" given what Musk did with DOGE. "No one else had the bandwidth to do what he's done, to identify what he's done."

Write to Olivia Beavers at Olivia.Beavers@wsj.com and Jasmine Li at jasmine.li@wsj.com

data.title: Mitsui-Soko Holdings Profit Falls 17% in Fiscal 2025
data.description:
Mitsui-Soko Holdings profit attributable to owners of the parent fell 17% to over 10 billion yen for fiscal 2025 from 12.1 billion yen a year earlier.

The marine cargo handling company's basic earnings per share (EPS) fell to 134.25 yen from 162.07 yen a year ago, according to a Tokyo bourse filing on May 9.

Operating revenue rose 7.7% to 280.7 billion yen from 260.6 billion yen a year earlier. It declared a final dividend of 73 yen per share for the fiscal 2025, payable from June 6.

For fiscal 2026, the company expects attributable profit of 10.2 billion yen, EPS of 136.35 yen, and revenue of 294 billion yen. The company expects to pay interim and year-end dividends of 24.5 yen per share, each, for the current fiscal year.

data.title: Malaysia Bubble-Tea Chain Operator Files IPO Prospectus
data.description:
By Ying Xian Wong

KUALA LUMPUR, Malaysia--Beverage chain operator Loob has submitted a draft prospectus for an initial public offering in Malaysia, as the latest company eager to capitalize on growing investor appetite for risk.

The company, which owns food-and-beverage brands including Tealive and Bask Bear, plans to sell a total of 350.8 million shares, according to the prospectus.

The draft prospectus didn't disclose the financial terms of the offer, but people familiar with the process said earlier that the offering could raise up to $150 million.

Investor interest in the Malaysian market is growing, driven by improving sentiment and a steady listing pipeline. Global equity markets have stabilized after months of heightened volatility due to ebbing trade tensions.

Last year, listings in Malaysia raised a total of $1.7 billion, the highest among Southeast Asian markets. According to the local stock exchange, 55 companies went public on Bursa Malaysia in 2024, the most in 19 years.

So far this year, 26 companies have listed on the exchange.

Loob operates over 831 Tealive bubble-tea outlets and more than 135 Bask Bear coffee stores in Malaysia. It has more than 100 overseas locations in markets such as Mauritius and Canada.

The company plans to use the IPO proceeds to repay debt and expand the business. Loob's net profit for the fiscal year ended June 30, 2024, was 50.8 million ringgit, equivalent to $12 million, up from 38.7 million ringgit for the prior year.

Maybank Investment Bank and AmInvestment Bank are advising on the IPO.

Write to Ying Xian Wong at yingxian.wong@wsj.com

data.title: Evolution Mining Says Group Mineral Resources Estimated at 30 Million Ounces of Gold, 4.4 Million Tonnes of Copper
data.description:
Evolution Mining said its group mineral resources are estimated to contain 30 million ounces of gold and 4.4 million tonnes of copper, while its group ore reserves are estimated to contain 11 million ounces of gold and 1.4 million tonnes of copper, representing an average mine life of around 15 years as of Dec. 31, 2024, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The mineral resources estimate represents growth in contained gold at its Mungari, Ernest Henry, and Northparkes operations, the filing said.

It represents a net increase of 370,000 ounces in contained gold and 130,000 tonnes in contained copper, compared to the December 2023 estimate at the Ernest Henry operations, as well as a net increase of 1.3 million ounces in contained gold at the Mungari operations.

data.title: Midea Compressor Recognized: KOLs' Teardown Videos Expose Aircons' Core Component
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR, Malaysia, June 05, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Recently, influencers from Thailand and Malaysia shared teardown reviews of air conditioners, sparking widespread discussion. Many were surprised to learn that top Japanese brands like Panasonic and Daikin use “GMCC” compressors—manufactured by Midea, the world’s No.1 residential inverter AC company.

Malaysian Tech DIYer Berani Buat disassembled Daikin and Panasonic units and found the GMCC label inside. His video drew attention, with viewers surprised by a Chinese-made core part in a Japanese-branded product. Meanwhile, Thai creators like Daddy Tips , Lungchang, BT Beartai and Extreme IT also shared similar findings and demonstrated how the core compressor helps AC to cool down. These reviews challenge perceptions of brand origin and highlight Midea’s role not just as a supplier, but as a global innovator in air conditioning—quietly powering ACs with cutting-edge compressor technology.

Compressor: The “Heart” of Inverter Air Conditioners

The compressor is the most critical component in an air conditioning system, directly influencing cooling efficiency and energy consumption. Inverter technology optimizes the compressor's speed according to room temperature fluctuations, significantly improving energy efficiency compared to traditional fixed-speed air conditioners. Midea has become a key player in the supply chain, not only powering its own air conditioners but also providing compressors to other international brands such as Panasonic and Daikin.

Technological Superiority: Energy Efficiency and Performance

Midea’s self-developed inverter compressor delivers up to 50% energy savings over traditional models and 20% over standard inverters. Its AI algorithm analyzes indoor temperatures to reduce fluctuations, boosting comfort and efficiency. Paired with the AI ECOMASTER, which uses big data to adjust in real time, it offers ultra-precise ±0.3°C control. This minimizes unnecessary changes and cuts energy waste. Verified by SGS to achieve up to 30% additional savings, Midea leads in smart, energy-efficient air conditioning technology.

Southeast Asia's Ideal Solution: Localized Design and Excellent Durability

Midea’s advanced compressor and inverter technology deliver strong cooling, quiet performance, and energy efficiency ideal for Southeast Asia’s hot climate. Designed for regional challenges, Midea air conditioners feature Prime Guard with 7 key protections, including TU1 copper tubes, Silver Shield (0.1% corrosion), Hyper Grapfins™ (12.5x corrosion resistance), and UV PCB protection. With wide voltage support (80V–265V) and insect resistance, these innovations ensure durability, stability, and long-term performance in humid, demanding environments.

Global Recognition: The Smart Choice for Southeast Asia

Midea’s technological leadership has gained global recognition, serving over 400 million users in 200 countries. In 2022, it exported 8.3 million inverter air conditioners to Southeast Asia, contributing significantly to regional growth. This success is backed by over $2 billion in R&D investment over five years. At the core is Midea’s GMCC compressor, which powers both its own products and premium international brands, offering top-tier cooling and energy efficiency. Leveraging this advanced technology, Midea delivers powerful, energy-saving, and comfortable air conditioning—making it the smart choice for Southeast Asian consumers seeking reliability and performance.

Midea Official Website

  • Thailand https://www.midea.com/th
  • Malaysia https://www.midea.com/my
  • Indonesia https://www.midea.com/id
  • Vietnam https://www.midea.com/vn
  • The Philippines https://www.midea.com/ph

data.title: FDI Into Vietnam Rises 7.9% in January-May
data.description:
Foreign direct investment (FDI) in Vietnam rose 7.9% year-on-year to USD 8.90 billion from January to May 2025.

FDI pledges—an indicator of future disbursements—reached USD 18.39 billion, marking a 51.2% surge compared to the same period last year.

In the first five months, manufacturing and processing sectors attracted the most foreign investment at USD 10.39 billion (56.5% of total), followed by real estate with USD 4.99 billion (27.1%).

Science and technology drew USD 1.02 billion, while wholesale and retail brought in over USD 596.8 million.

By source, Singapore led with over USD 4.38 billion, representing 23.8% of total foreign inflows and a 30.1% year-on-year increase.

South Korea ranked second with more than USD 2.93 billion—nearly 16% of the total and 2.47 times higher than the same period last year.

data.title: China Stocks Wobble After Trump-Xi Call
data.description:
The Shanghai Composite and Shenzhen Component indexes traded around the flatline on Friday, struggling for clear direction as investors assessed the phone call between US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping.

The two leaders agreed to continue negotiations as the latest dispute over rare earths appeared to have cooled, though no concrete details were provided.

Despite the diplomatic overture, market sentiment remained cautious amid expectations that geopolitical tensions will persist as the US and China continue to test each other’s economic resilience.

Investors now turn their attention to upcoming Chinese inflation and trade data, due Monday, for fresh signals on the health of the world’s second-largest economy.

Notable gainers included Avic Chengdu (+6.3%), Hengbao Co (+2.2%), and Tianjin Benefo (+10%), while Shenzhen Forms (-5.0%) and Talkweb Information (-2.2%) posted significant losses.

For the week, both major indexes are on track to gain over 1%.

data.title: Gold Rises Ahead of US Jobs Report
data.description:
Gold rose to around $3,360 per ounce on Friday, trimming losses from the previous session, as soft US economic data and expectations of less restrictive Federal Reserve lifted the metal’s safety appeal ahead of nonfarm payroll release.

Initial jobless claims rose to its highest level since early October 2024 in late May, surpassing expectations.

First-quarter productivity was also revised lower, while the trade deficit narrowed due to falling imports.

Earlier in the week, the ADP report showed May’s smallest job gain since March 2023, and the ISM Services PMI signaled the sector’s first contraction in nearly a year.

Meanwhile, paring some gains was renewed optimism over easing trade tensions after US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping reached an agreement to hold further discussions though no concrete details were provided.

Over the week, bullion is poised for a weekly gain of around 2%.

data.title: Copper Flat; Signs of Tight Supply Linger — Market Talk
data.description: Copper is flat in early Asian trade. There are ongoing signs of supply tightness, say ANZ research analysts in a commentary. The decline in inventories in London Metal Exchange warehouses is continuing with orders for another 14 kilo tonnes from facilities in South Korea and the Netherlands, they note. Meanwhile, market sentiment has been boosted by the easing of trade tensions, thanks to a conversation between U.S. President Trump and Chinese President Xi. The three-month LME copper contract is flat at $9,740 a ton. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

data.title: Australian Dollar Weakens
data.description:
The Australian dollar weakened toward $0.650 on Friday, as dovish RBA outweighed cautious optimism over US-China trade negotiations.The Reserve Bank of Australia’s May meeting minutes showed policymakers saw a stronger case for a 25 basis point cut, emphasizing a cautious, predictable stance.

RBA Assistant Governor Sarah Hunter warned that higher US tariffs and rising uncertainty could dampen global growth and weigh on Australia’s economy.

Meanwhile, President Trump and China’s Xi Jinping held a phone call on Thursday, where both sides agreed to resume trade talks aimed at ending the ongoing trade war.

While this is positive for the global economy, analysts noted that tensions remain high amid a lack of substantive progress.

They also flagged the August 12 expiry of the 90-day pause in the US-China trade war as a key downside risk for the Australian dollar.

However, the Aussie found support as rising odds for additional Fed rate cuts kept the US dollar under pressure.

WEC Energy Group Prices Upsized $775M of 3.375% Convertible Senior Notes Due 2028 >WEC
Thai baht/US dollar weaker on Friday
data.description:
The Thai baht weakened against the U.S. dollar on Friday.

At 0218 GMT, the baht was 0.09% lower at 32.65 versus the dollar, after trading in a range of 32.585 to 32.690. It ended the previous session at 32.62 per dollar, as per LSEG data.

data.title: Fulcrum Lithium Starts Phase Two Drilling at Nevada Project
data.description:
Fulcrum Lithium started its phase two drill program at the Alkali Flats project in Nevada to follow up on the lithium mineralization system discovered in phase one, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

Initial assay results from the 19-hole program are expected in July, the filing said.

The company will begin the maiden drilling at the Fairway project after completing the drilling program at Alkali Flats, per the filing.
